steps.title: Notice the water contrast
steps.content: The poem contrasts the storm outside Puerto Cabello with the sheltered waters near the town. The tempest is "raving" outside, while the local waters move easily and the pirogue glides gently at the dip of the oar.

steps.title: Reject shifted focuses
steps.content: A shifts to daily life, C wrongly says the storm is expected to arrive in Puerto Cabello, and D focuses on boats instead of water conditions. The main purpose is to contrast calm local waters with rougher waters elsewhere. The answer is B.
